rationale: Both correctly land on the maintainer's actual resolution (duplicate of #4808, per #4809). Y is more grounded — it shows an actual file read and grep confirming the get_cache_scale call is already gone from hunyuan_image3_transformer.py, quotes the real comment left in its place, and gives the reporter concrete next steps (update to main or apply the one-line workaround). X is more speculative: it asserts specific unverified GitHub metadata ('#4808 mergedAt is null') without showing any evidence-gathering step, which reads as embellishment risk even though its rebase-branch-vs-main caveat is a thoughtful, contextually relevant catch. X's added test-coverage-gap claim is plausible but similarly unverified in the transcript. Y is also more directly useful to the reporter's actual situation (unblocking now) versus X's process-level flags aimed at maintainers.
